# @stll/regex-set

> NAPI-RS bindings to Rust regex-automata for
> multi-pattern regex matching in Node.js and Bun.
> Single pass, O(m*n) guaranteed, no backtracking.

Status: public release preparation in progress.
Until the first npm release lands, build from source:

  bun install
  bun run build
  bun run build:js

## Quick start

```typescript
import { RegexSet } from "@stll/regex-set";

const rs = new RegexSet([
  "\\d{2}\\.\\d{2}\\.\\d{4}",
  "\\+?\\d{9,12}",
  "[A-Z]{2}\\d{6}",
]);

rs.findIter("Born 15.03.1990, ID CZ123456");
// [{ pattern: 0, ..., text: "15.03.1990" },
//  { pattern: 2, ..., text: "CZ123456" }]

rs.replaceAll(text, ["[DATE]", "[PHONE]", "[ID]"]);
```

## API

Constructor:
`new RegexSet(patterns: PatternEntry[], options?: Options)`

PatternEntry:
- `string`
- `RegExp`
- `{ pattern: string | RegExp; name?: string }`

Options:
- `wholeWords`: `boolean` (default `false`, wraps patterns with `\b...\b`)
- `unicodeBoundaries`: `boolean` (default `true`)

Methods:
- `findIter(haystack: string): Match[]`
- `isMatch(haystack: string): boolean`
- `whichMatch(haystack: string): number[]`
- `replaceAll(haystack: string, replacements: string[]): string`
- `patternCount: number`

Match type (same as @stll/aho-corasick):
```typescript
type Match = {
  pattern: number;
  start: number;
  end: number;
  text: string;
};
```

Offsets are UTF-16 code units, compatible with
`String.prototype.slice()`.

## Lookaround

Lookahead (`(?=...)`, `(?!...)`) and lookbehind
(`(?<=...)`, `(?<!...)`) are supported. Internally,
assertions are stripped from patterns, cores are
compiled into a single DFA, and assertions are
verified as inline char checks (~1ns per match).
Complex assertions fall back to fancy-regex on a
small window.

## Important notes

- Guaranteed O(m * n). No catastrophic backtracking.
- Rust regex syntax: no backreferences.
- `\d`, `\w`, `\s` are Unicode-aware by default.
- `replaceAll` throws if `replacements.length !== patternCount`.
- For pure literal patterns, use @stll/aho-corasick instead (faster for literals).
- Composable with @stll/aho-corasick (same Match type, same UTF-16 offsets).
- For browsers, use the separate `@stll/regex-set-wasm` package.
